texto: She loved the smell of books, the feel of books, the look of them on the shelf.

texto: Who are we really? Combinations of common chemicals that perform mechanical actions for a few years before crumbling back into the original components? Fresh new souls, drawn at random for some celestial cupboard where God keeps an unending supply?Or the same soul, immortal and eternal, refurbished and reused through endless lives, by that thrifty Housekeeper? In Her wisdom and benevolence She wipes off the memory slates, as part of the cleaning process, because if we could remember all the things we have experienced in earlier lives, we might object to risking it again.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Barbara Michaels que provoca sentido da existência e experiência vivida. Ao questionar “Who are we really? Combinations of common chemicals that perform mechanical actions for a few yea…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, experience, past-lives, com foco em termino e past-lives.

texto: Probe deeply enough, under the slickest façade of confidence, and you tapped a vein of self-doubt or a hidden fear. Irrational fears and baseless doubts, many of them, but that was precisely why constant reassurance was necessary to the human animal.
explicacao: Citação de Barbara Michaels sobre enfrentar incertezas com ação. “Probe deeply enough, under the slickest façade of confidence, and you tapped a vein of self-doubt…” articula autoestima, confidence, fear em registro de autoestima, com relevância para contextos como fear e self-doubt.
